publication.changeSummary: Misconception MCQ, canonical fill-gap, and no code task for Compiler and runtime diagnostics; every item remains blocked from formal exams pending independent human review.

items.title: Compiler and runtime diagnostics: reject the misconception
items.prompt: Which statement is source-aligned for Java: Beginner to Intermediate and corrects this misconception? Every Java defect becomes either a compiler error or a stack trace, so a run without either must be correct.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted draft item; independent human source, technical, pedagogy, accessibility, and answer-oracle review is required before formal-exam eligibility.

hints: Compare each option with both the language-specific boundary case and the cited operational model.

feedback.correct: Correct. Classify compile-time, exceptional runtime, and logic failures separately; start with the first causal diagnostic or relevant application stack frame and verify the repaired contract.
feedback.incorrect: That choice preserves the misconception. Classify compile-time, exceptional runtime, and logic failures separately; start with the first causal diagnostic or relevant application stack frame and verify the repaired contract.

criteria.description: Selects the source-aligned correction and rejects the documented misconception.

privateAuthorNotes: A human reviewer must verify source fidelity and ensure the distractor remains plausible without teaching it as fact.
